text: To set effective project goals, use the SMART framework: goals should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of "improve the website," aim for "increase page load speed by 20% within three months." Break the project into smaller milestones, such as completing a prototype or testing phases. Each milestone should have clear deliverables, like a report or a working feature. It is also crucial to check stakeholder alignment—ensure everyone involved agrees on the goals and priorities. This avoids confusion later. Regular reviews help you track progress and adjust if needed. With clear goals, milestones, and stakeholder support, your project has a solid foundation.
